Title: Govt warns firms against 'channel switching' fuel abuse
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan has issued a stern warning to corporations and industrial players against "channel switching", where subsidised retail fuel is exploited to avoid paying market rates.
He said some companies had been discreetly purchasing diesel at the RM6.72 per litre retail pump price instead of the RM7 to RM8 per litre industrial rate, effectively taking advantage of government-absorbed subsidies.
"The government is warning these companies to stop channel switching activities immediately or face strict enforcement action," he said during the Global Energy Crisis Briefing, which was livestreamed on Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's official Facebook page.
Amir Hamzah said enforcement efforts were being stepped up at petrol stations and national borders to curb smuggling and ensure domestic fuel supply remains stable.
The briefing highlighted a global energy crunch triggered by conflict in the Middle East, which has disrupted the Strait of Hormuz, pushing shipping costs up threefold and causing logistics insurance premiums to surge up to 16-fold.
To shield consumers, the government is currently absorbing a fuel subsidy burden of RM6 billion, significantly higher than the RM700 million recorded prior to the conflict.
Under the Budi Madani (Budi95) programme, the subsidised RON95 price remains at RM1.99 per litre for the first 200 litres, compared with the unsubsidised retail price of RM4.27 per litre.
For diesel, the retail price in Peninsular Malaysia stands at RM6.72 per litre, while in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan it is maintained at RM2.15 per litre.
Amir Hamzah expressed appreciation to members of the public who have acted as the government's "eyes and ears" by reporting misconduct, including cases involving foreigners purchasing RON95 or other suspicious activities at petrol stations.
"This spirit of togetherness is what helps us address the crisis, and our strength in an uncertain world lies in our solidarity and moderation," he said.
On Malaysia's economic outlook, he said the country entered 2026 on strong footing, supported by robust domestic demand and the ringgit's performance as one of Asia's stronger currencies.
He attributed the resilience to three years of fiscal discipline and targeted subsidy reforms, which have created fiscal space to absorb current global shocks.
"Today, Malaysia has demonstrated the stability and competitiveness that continues to attract foreign investors as a preferred destination," he added.
Amir Hamzah urged Malaysians to practise energy conservation and support local products to help safeguard national resources amid global uncertainty.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sunway International Schools host Open Day with experiential classes, tours
Article:
SUNWAY International Schools (SIS) is hosting an Open Day on April 18, offering parents and students a chance to experience its international education programmes firsthand.
The event will be held at both Sunway International School Kuala Lumpur (SISKL) and Sunway International School Iskandar Puteri (SIS Iskandar).
Sunway International Schools Open Day
Date: April 18 (Saturday)
Time: 10am to 3pm
TWO CAMPUSES, ONE WORLD-CLASS EDUCATION
SIS offers world-class education from kindergarten to pre-university, providing three internationally recognised pathways: Canadian, Cambridge and International Baccalaureate (IB).
The school focuses on developing academic excellence and life-long values in a dynamic, global environment.
SISKL partners with Magdalen College School, Oxford, while SIS Iskandar is Johor's only Canadian IB continuum school, offering a unique, inquiry-driven learning experience.
WHAT TO EXPECT
Besides diving into programme offerings, the Open Day will feature experiential classes, student exhibitions and hands-on activities for the whole family.
Attendees can enjoy a carnival-style atmosphere with food and beverage stations, mini games, balloon art and fundraising booths.
Families can also tour SISKL's 3.6-hectare GreenRE Platinum-certified campus or SIS Iskandar's modern campus, set amidst nature and innovation.
The Open Day provides a chance to meet the admissions team and have questions answered directly.
Attendees can enjoy registration fee benefits up to RM9,000* for SISKL and RM8,000 for SIS Iskandar.
Book a spot for
SISKL
or
SIS Iskandar
's upcoming Open Day on April 18!
*Terms and conditions apply.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vietnamese man pleads guilty to possessing python bile and tongues
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU: A Vietnamese man pleaded guilty at the Sessions Court here today to two charges of possessing more than 1,000 python parts without a licence, involving a protected wildlife species.
Hoang Van Thai, 39, entered his plea after the charges were read out in Vietnamese before Judge Madihah Zainol.
He was charged with possessing 1,022 python bile and 191 python tongue parts from the Python reticulatus species at a premises in Taman Johor Jaya at about 9.50pm on April 4.
He was charged under Section 60(1)(b) of the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 (Act 716), read with Section 34 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum fine of RM50,000, up to three years' jail, or both upon conviction.
Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) prosecuting officer Mas Izzaty Lokman objected to bail, citing the seriousness of the offence and the large quantity of seized items, which suggested an organised activity.
The court was informed that the total value of the seized items was estimated at RM1,037,215.
The prosecution said the accused posed a flight risk as he was still in possession of a valid travel document, an international student escort pass.
However, defence lawyer Mohd Fazaly Ali Mohd Ghazaly applied for bail, saying his client had family dependents in Malaysia, including a wife and two children who were studying here.
He also argued that the valuation of the seized items was not supported by documentation, and that the charge only involved possession offences.
The court fixed April 20 for mention to verify the accused's immigration status before deciding on the bail application.  — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Artemis astronauts to shed light on space health risks
Article:
WASHINGTON: While the Artemis II astronauts have been protected from the icy vacuum of space on their journey, their bodies have nonetheless been left exposed to possibly high levels of radiation – a danger of space travel that Nasa is anxiously waiting to study.
Their trip around the Moon has taken the four astronauts farther into space than any human before – more than 1,000 times the distance from Earth to the International Space Station.
Earth's magnetosphere offers some protection against radioactive cosmic rays and solar particles to the orbiting ISS, but no such cover on the Moon.
Studying the impacts of radiation is essential as Nasa hopes to eventually build a Moon base and send astronauts on the long trip to Mars.
The US space agency installed radiation sensors on the Orion capsule and took blood samples of the astronauts before takeoff to compare with samples post-trip. The crew's saliva samples are gathered throughout the journey and their health is monitored via smartwatches.
Nasa has also placed state-of-the-art computer chips in the capsule that can replicate certain physiological functions, like that of an organ.
Mission planners chose to mimic bone marrow, which produces blood cells and is one of the tissues "most quickly affected by radiation and other stressors," Nasa Human Research Programme chief scientist Steven Platts told AFP.
With all of the data, Platts said his team hoped to better understand the variation between low earth orbit and deep space.
"It will be good research information for us to see the level of radiation, but also the type of radiation," he said.
[[nid:1414778]]
"Our prediction is that we'll see a lot more galactic cosmic radiation... which is from supernovas and is everywhere," versus radiation coming from the Sun, he said.
- Mental health -
Platts said while most people associate radiation with cancer risk, it also can impact the central nervous system and blood circulation.
"It can lead to inflammation in the brain, and that's one of the things that can increase the risk of Parkinson's disease," he said.
With the Artemis II astronauts spending just 10 days in space, radiation is not a major concern, but the danger could rise dramatically with longer stays on the Moon.
Extensive health studies were conducted on the Apollo astronauts, but technology has advanced greatly in the 50 years since, said Bruce Betts, chief scientist at the Planetary Society, a space advocacy group.
"There will be a lot of information on the medical side of things," he said.
The other major focus is on psychological risks of spending long periods of time on the Moon or Mars.
With greater disconnection from home and tight living quarters, mental health could pose the greatest danger to astronauts on such missions, Platts said.
He compared the difference between the ISS and the tiny Orion capsule as like going from a "six-bedroom house, like a mansion... to a camper van." - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police cripple gang behind armed robberies in Penang
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Police have crippled a gang believed to be behind a string of armed robberies in Penang following a series of raids under Op Rantau Kongsi on April 9 and April 10.
A team from the Penang police contingent headquarters Criminal Investigation Department carried out coordinated operations at several locations in Chemor, Perak, and Seberang Prai Tengah, Penang.
A total of 11 foreigners — nine men and two women, aged between 22 and 38 — were detained for suspected involvement in gang robberies using machetes.
Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said police also seized items believed to belong to victims, including jewellery, mobile phones and cash amounting to RM2,770.
He said weapons allegedly used in the crimes, including machetes, knives and cable ties, were also recovered.
"Investigations revealed that the suspects targeted foreign nationals staying at workers' accommodation on mainland Penang.
"The group would carry out robberies armed with machetes, wearing face masks and restraining victims using cable ties," he said today.
Azizee said the arrests led to the resolution of a gang robbery reported at about 1.30am on April 9 in Seberang Prai Selatan, involving losses estimated at RM35,000 in jewellery, mobile phones of various brands and cash.
"We believe the group is linked to five gang robbery cases — one in Seberang Prai Utara, two in Seberang Prai Tengah and two in Seberang Prai Selatan," he added.
The case is being investigated under Sections 395 and 397 of the Penal Code for gang robbery and armed robbery.
Checks showed one of the suspects has a prior immigration-related offence, while the others have no previous criminal records. All 11 tested negative for drugs.
Earlier today, the Jawi magistrate's court issued a seven-day
remand
order against the suspects, including an Indonesian couple.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms across KL, Putrajaya and 8 states until 7pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, as well as eight states, until 7pm today.
In a statement, MetMalaysia said the adverse weather was expected to cover the whole state of Perlis, as well as several areas in Kedah, namely Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena and Padang Terap.
The same warning has also been issued for Perak (Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim), Pahang (Cameron Highlands and Jerantut) and Selangor (Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang).
In Negri Sembilan, the affected areas are Jelebu, Seremban, Kuala Pilah and Rembau.
Meanwhile, in Sarawak, the warning covers Serian, Samarahan (Simunjan), Sarikei (Pakan, Julau and Meradong), Sibu, Mukah (Daro, Matu, Dalat and Mukah), Kapit (Song and Kapit), Bintulu and Miri (Subis, Beluru and Marudi).
In Sabah, the affected areas include the Interior (Beaufort, Keningau and Tambunan), West Coast, Tawau, Sandakan (Telupid and Beluran) and Kudat (Kota Marudu and Pitas).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mobile messaging jumps 240pct as chat-based commerce rises in Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is seeing a faster-than-average shift towards conversational commerce, with total platform interactions rising 17 per cent in 2025, according to Infobip's Messaging Trends Report 2026.
Mobile messaging app interactions surged 240 per cent during the year, while WhatsApp usage rose 40 per cent and Viber increased 107 per cent.
Voice and video interactions grew 38 per cent, and email rose 20 per cent, signalling rising demand for multi-channel communication strategies.
Infobip said the findings were based on 628 billion mobile interactions recorded in 2025, alongside a historical dataset of 3.8 trillion messages over the past 20 years.
The data highlights a broader shift from single-channel messaging to artificial intelligence (AI)-powered omnichannel engagement, as businesses adopt richer conversational platforms.
Short message service continues to account for 62 per cent of global messaging traffic due to its reliability.
In the Asia-Pacific region, rich communication services (RCS) traffic rose more than 500 per cent over the past year, while WhatsApp accounted for 91 per cent of conversational AI interactions on Infobip's platform.
Infobip vice-president and general manager for Asia Pacific, Ruslana Reznikova, said the sharp rise in mobile messaging interactions in Malaysia shows consumers increasingly prefer to engage with brands on the same platforms they use to communicate with friends and family.
"As we move into the era of agentic AI, the goal for businesses across Asia Pacific is to transition from simple notifications to meaningful, two-way conversations that drive loyalty and measurable business outcomes," she said.
Infobip chief revenue officer Ante Pamuković said the company's 20-year dataset offers a unique perspective on how the industry is evolving.
"The story is clear, the era of the simple notification is over. We are shifting towards a diverse ecosystem where brands orchestrate conversations across WhatsApp, RCS, email and voice.
"The future is omnichannel, conversational, and increasingly powered by agentic AI.
"In this new landscape, Infobip provides the infrastructure enabling businesses to reach customers on the right channel, at the right time, with the right message," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang ambulance crash fuels debate over road safety at junctions
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: An ambulance collided with a car at a busy traffic light junction near Universiti Sains Malaysia in Penang, sparking debate over road safety.
The incident, believed to have occurred at about
11.03am on April 9 in Gelugor,
was captured on dashcam and shared on social media.
Footage shared online by a Facebook user identified as @NorFazlan appears to show the moments before the crash, with the ambulance entering the intersection before colliding with a Perodua Axia.
The impact brought both vehicles to a stop, drawing the attention of nearby motorists and bystanders.
The video has since sparked discussion online, with many highlighting the risks faced by emergency vehicles at busy intersections.
In the accompanying post, commenters, including @NasrulHaq and @CheRosliOmar, urged ambulance drivers to exercise caution at traffic lights in high-traffic areas, noting that modern vehicles are more soundproof and often fitted with tinted windows.
Another user, @JanganTanya, also urged the public to be cautious and reduce speed when approaching junctions, adding that such incidents can happen unexpectedly.
It was not immediately known if anyone was injured in the collision.
The New Straits Times has reached out to the police for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Explainer: Five things to know about the US-Iran talks in Islamabad
Article:
ISLAMABAD: The United States and Iran are holding their highest-level talks in years in Islamabad in a Pakistan-brokered bid to turn a fragile two-week ceasefire into a lasting end to a war that has roiled global energy markets.
Here are five things to know about the Islamabad talks:
- The war behind the talks -
On Feb 28, the US and Israel launched coordinated strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and struck Iran's military and nuclear infrastructure, killing more than 2,000 people in five weeks.
Teheran responded by effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz, through which about a fifth of global oil and gas passes, sending energy prices soaring and disrupting trade worldwide.
On April 8, the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan. Iran's Supreme National Security Council said the talks could continue for up to 15 days. The ceasefire is expected to expire April 22.
- Pakistan's unlikely starring role -
Pakistan, a nation more frequently making international headlines for its militancy and shaky economy, is hosting the first negotiations between Washington and Tehran since the war began, a stunning pivot for a country long viewed through the lens of deep security concerns.
The country does not typically host talks of this scale.
Pakistan's value as mediator rests on an unusually broad diplomatic network.
Iran was the first country to recognise Pakistan following independence in 1947, with the two neighbours sharing a 900-kilometre (560-mile) border and deep historical, cultural and religious ties. Pakistan is also home to over 20 million Shia Muslims: the second-largest such population in the world after Iran.
At the same time, Islamabad has cultivated strong ties with Washington, Riyadh and Beijing.
Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar visited Beijing at the end of March for talks with Wang Yi, who backed Islamabad's mediation efforts as "in keeping with the common interests of all parties."
Trump himself told AFP that China helped bring Iran to the negotiating table, an account backed by Pakistani officials.
"On ceasefire night, hopes were fading, but China stepped in and convinced Iran to agree to a preliminary ceasefire," a senior Pakistani official familiar with the negotiations told AFP on condition of anonymity.
[[nid:1414786]]
"While our efforts were central, we were falling short of a breakthrough, which was ultimately achieved after Beijing persuaded the Iranians."
- What's on the table? -
The gap between the two sides remains vast.
Washington's reported 15-point proposal centres on Iran's enriched uranium, ballistic missiles, sanctions relief and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran has countered with a 10-point plan demanding control over the strait, a toll for vessels crossing the strait, an end to all regional military operations and the lifting of all sanctions.
Lebanon is also a major sticking point. Israel continued its strikes in the country targeting Hizbollah – after the ceasefire came into force – with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu rejecting Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif's assertion that the truce included Lebanon.
US Vice President JD Vance appeared to take a softer tone, saying there may have been a "legitimate misunderstanding" from Iran that Lebanon would be included.
Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian warned on X that Israel's strikes on Lebanon rendered the negotiations "meaningless." "Our hands remain on the trigger. Iran will never forsake its Lebanese brothers and sisters.
- Who are the negotiators? -
Vance will lead the American team, joined by special envoy Steve Witkoff and presidential son-in-law Jared Kushner.
It marks the most senior US engagement with Iran since Secretary of State John Kerry negotiated the 2015 nuclear deal. Witkoff held multiple rounds of Oman-mediated talks with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi before the war cut the process short.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Araghchi are expected to lead the Iranian delegation.
Ghalibaf is a former IRGC commander, and it remains unclear whether any active IRGC representative will attend.
- Islamabad on lockdown -
The talks are being held in the Pakistani capital Islamabad.
The government has kept its cards close to its chest, without confirming the venue. Pakistan's leader Sharif had said the talks would take place on Friday, while the White House said the first round would begin on Saturday.
The Serena Hotel, located next to the foreign ministry in the capital's high-security Red Zone, asked its guests to clear out on Wednesday, and that same day authorities in the capital announced a two-day public holiday on Thursday and Friday.
The talks themselves are expected to be indirect: the two delegations sitting in separate rooms with Pakistani officials shuttling proposals between them, mirroring the format used in earlier Oman-mediated rounds.
Outside, the streets of Islamabad are flooded with armed security personnel in military fatigues, traffic diversions and police checkpoints. The capital, already a quiet city, was even quieter on Friday. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Porsche deliveries tumble further in first quarter
Article:
BERLIN: Porsche AG's deliveries slumped further in the first three months of 2026, with sharp declines in key markets China and the United States, as loses its shine across.
Global deliveries were down 15 per cent in the first quarter at 60,991 vehicles, the German sports car maker said on Friday.
In China, once a major growth engine for the Stuttgart-based carmaker, deliveries fell by 21 per cent amid stiff competition on pricing and technology from local brands.
Porsche reported a 10 per cent drop in deliveries to North America, driven partly by the discontinuation of US tax incentives for electric vehicles, according to a company statement.
Its home market Germany was the only region that saw growth at four per cent. Deliveries in the rest of Europe plunged 18 per cent.
Porsche pivoted back to combustion engine models and delayed the launch of some all-electric vehicles last year as demand sagged, at a cost of 1.8 billion euros (US$2.1 billion) to earnings.
New chief executive officer Michael Leiters has pledged a turnaround with ruthless cost-cutting and new models.
The first-quarter figures, which were impacted by the discontinuation of Porsche's combustion-engine 718 models and a strong prior-year period for the all-electric Macan, were "overall in line with our expectations", board member for sales Matthias Becker said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Toddler rescued after leg gets stuck in bathroom floor trap
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A two-year-old boy was rescued by firefighters after his leg became trapped in a bathroom floor trap at an apartment in Bandar Sri Damansara today.
The Selangor Fire and Rescue Department said it received an emergency call at 12.13pm, prompting the deployment of a team from the Sungai Buloh Fire and Rescue Station.
Its assistant director of operations, Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar, said the incident involved a toddler whose leg was lodged in a pipe drain beneath the bathroom floor trap of a unit at Pangsapuri Sri Meranti.
"Firefighters used special equipment to cut through the pipe and safely extricated the child," he said in a statement.
The fire engine arrived at the scene, about 9km from the station, at 12.23pm.
He said the operation was brought under control at 2.54pm and concluded at 2.58pm, with five personnel involved.
The child was subsequently handed over to Health Ministry personnel for further assessment and treatment, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dubai caps foreign flights until May 31, Indian carriers hardest hit
Article:
NEW DELHI: Dubai has restricted foreign airlines to just one daily flight to its airports until May 31 due to the Iran crisis, raising concerns over revenue losses among Indian carriers, which had planned more flights than airlines from any other country, according to letters seen by Reuters.
The Federation of Indian Airlines (FIA), which represents major carriers IndiGo, Air India and SpiceJet, has urged the Indian government to press Dubai authorities to lift the curbs. Failing that, it has called for reciprocal measures against Dubai-based airlines, including Emirates and flydubai, according to a March 31 letter.
Indian carriers are already under financial strain from higher fuel costs and longer routings to Western destinations, after being barred from using Pakistani airspace since last year amid military tensions between the two neighbours.
In a March 27 email to airlines, seen by Reuters, Dubai Airports said carriers would be limited to one return flight per day to Dubai International Airport (DXB) — typically the world's busiest international hub — and Al Maktoum International Airport (DWC) during the summer season from April 20 to May 31. The move extends restrictions imposed following the outbreak of the conflict.
"Carriers continue to be limited to one rotation per day until capacity allows for expansion… Additional slots will be allocated if capacity permits," it said.
The FIA said the curbs do not apply to Dubai-based airlines such as Emirates and flydubai, creating an uneven playing field that could result in "substantial" revenue losses.
Dubai Airports and the emirate's media office did not respond to requests for comment. Flydubai said its flight schedules were approved by the relevant authorities, while Emirates did not comment.
The measures come as Emirates and other Gulf carriers have long raised concerns over India's bilateral air service agreements, which cap seat capacity between countries. Indian authorities maintain that such agreements protect domestic airlines in a highly competitive market.
India was the largest source of passengers for DXB in 2025, with 11.9 million travellers passing through the hub.
The Dubai caps are expected to hit Indian carriers the hardest, based on April and May schedule data from Cirium.
Air India and its low-cost arm, Air India Express, had scheduled more than 750 flights into DXB during the period. IndiGo had planned 481 flights, followed by Saudia and Gulf Air with 480 and 404 respectively, while SpiceJet scheduled 61.
The one-flight-per-day cap would limit each foreign airline to about 30 or 31 flights per month — a sharp contrast to the hundreds operated by Emirates and flydubai, according to Flightradar24 data.
IndiGo said in a statement that the Middle East crisis and the extended Dubai restrictions had "significantly constrained" its operations, noting it had approval for 15 daily flights between India and Dubai.
"As a result, a significant portion of IndiGo's capacity and aircraft utilisation is currently underutilised," it said.
Air India, SpiceJet and Indian authorities did not respond to requests for comment.
Other major airlines, including Lufthansa, Singapore Airlines and British Airways, operated far fewer flights to Dubai prior to the crisis and have since suspended services to the city until at least May 31.
They are instead adding more non-stop Asia-Europe flights to tap strong passenger demand, which has driven up fares.
—Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump puts Vance in the hot seat to lead peace talks, end Iran war
Article:
WASHINGTON: It was a war JD Vance never wanted. Now the US vice president has been tasked with trying to end it.
Vance is heading to Pakistan this week under orders from President Donald Trump to turn a fragile Iran ceasefire into a lasting peace agreement.
For the 41-year-old vice president, who has kept a relatively low profile during the Middle East conflict, the assignment marks one of the biggest diplomatic tests of his career.
But the man widely seen as a potential contender in the 2028 US presidential race now faces major challenges as talks begin on Saturday in Islamabad.
"I cannot think of a case where the vice president ran formal negotiations like this," said Aaron Wolf Mannes, a lecturer at the University of Maryland School of Public Policy and an expert on the American vice presidency in foreign policy.
"This is high risk, high reward."
Vance built his political identity as an anti-interventionist, advocating for the US to avoid prolonged foreign wars such as Iraq, where he served as a US Marine.
That position has complicated his role after Trump launched the Iran war on February 28.
While Vance has publicly backed the conflict, he has largely remained out of the spotlight. When the ceasefire was announced, he was in Hungary supporting Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's election campaign.
The New York Times reported that in private discussions prior to the war, Vance had argued against military action, warning it could trigger regional instability and fracture Trump's MAGA coalition.
Now, however, he has emerged as Trump's key diplomatic envoy for a potential Iran deal.
"My key role was, I sat on the phone a lot," Vance told reporters as he left Hungary this week. "I answered a lot of phone calls. I made a lot of phone calls. And again, I'm happy about where we are."
Announcing the Islamabad talks, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said Vance had played a "very significant and key role" from the beginning.
Vance will be accompanied by special envoy Steve Witkoff and Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner, becoming the first US vice president to visit Pakistan since Joe Biden in 2011.
The White House said Vance, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Witkoff and Kushner have been working closely together on the Iran file.
"The President is optimistic that a deal can be reached that leads to lasting peace in the Middle East," said Principal Deputy Press Secretary Anna Kelly.
One theory is that Iran may view Vance as a more acceptable interlocutor given his reported scepticism of the war and broader US interventionism.
After Tehran expressed anger over continued Israeli strikes on Lebanese cities despite the ceasefire, Vance appeared to soften his tone, suggesting there may have been a "legitimate misunderstanding" over whether Lebanon was included.
He has not always struck a diplomatic tone.
A long-time sceptic of US support for Ukraine, Vance notably triggered an Oval Office clash between Trump and President Volodymyr Zelensky in February 2025.
For the ambitious Vance — a soon-to-be father of four and Catholic convert — politics remains ever-present.
Since Trump's return to power, Vance has taken on an unusually prominent role in a position that is both a heartbeat away from the presidency and, as one former vice president once said, "not worth a bucket of warm spit."
His role in the Iran talks comes amid speculation over a potential internal Republican rivalry with Rubio ahead of the 2028 election.
"If he can get something that papers it over without addressing the core issues, that's probably enough," said Mannes.
"But if nothing good comes of this, it raises questions about his competence — which is not going to help him electorally. And Rubio is right there as a potential rival for 2028."
—AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Langkawi ferry fares up RM10 for non-MyKad passengers
Article:
ALOR SETAR: Langkawi Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd has announced a RM10 fare increase for non-MyKad passengers, effective April 1.
Its general manager, Dr Baharin Baharom, said the adjustment is necessary due to sustained increases in industry operating costs that the company can no longer absorb.
"Over the years, the ferry industry has experienced significant increases in fuel prices, maintenance, manpower, and regulatory compliance costs, while Langkawi ferry fares have not reflected these rising operational expenses.
"Continuing operations under these conditions is no longer viable. Without this adjustment, the industry risks service reductions, operational disruptions, and long-term sustainability challenges that would ultimately affect users," he said in a statement.
After the adjustment, non-MyKad passenger fares from Kuala Perlis to Langkawi will be RM45 one way for adults and RM35 for children. From Kuala Kedah, fares will be RM55 for adults and RM42 for children.
Dr Baharin said the adjustment does not affect Malaysian MyKad holders, with affordability and accessibility for locals remaining a priority.
Fares for Malaysian citizens remain unchanged at RM21 (adults) and RM16 (children) from Kuala Perlis, and RM26.50 (adults) and RM20.50 (children) from Kuala Kedah.
Senior citizens are charged RM13 from Kuala Perlis and RM17 from Kuala Kedah, while persons with disabilities (PwD) pay RM10 and RM14 respectively.
He said the revised fares for non-MyKad passengers remain reasonable, noting that Langkawi ferry prices are still lower than comparable island routes in Malaysia.
"For comparison, ferry fares to Tioman are RM85 despite similar nautical distance. This demonstrates that even after the adjustment, Langkawi ferry fares remain well below prevailing market levels," he said.
He added that operational continuity and safety standards will not be compromised, stressing that the adjustment is necessary to ensure ferry services remain safe, reliable and sustainable.
—Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GOF arrests woman for selling LPG cylinders without permit
Article:
KOTA BARU: The General Operations Force (GOF) has arrested a woman for selling liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) cylinders without approval from the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry on Tuesday.
GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the 58-year-old woman was detained at Kampung Baguh in Rantau Panjang.
He said the raid was carried out at a grocery shop by a GOF team from the Ninth Battalion.
"Acting on intelligence, the team raided the premises at 2.45pm.
"Upon arrival, the team found the woman at the payment counter before identifying themselves and requesting her identification documents.
"A further search of the premises led to the discovery of 15 LPG cylinders of various brands and colours, all filled with gas," he said.
Ahmad Radzi said the woman failed to produce any valid documents or permits for the storage and sale of the controlled items.
"The suspect was arrested and all 15 LPG cylinders were seized before being brought to the GOF base in Taman Bakti for documentation and further action," he said.
The seized items comprised 10 Petronas LPG cylinders (14kg), three Petron 12kg cylinders and two BHP 12kg cylinders, with a total estimated value of RM1,899.
Ahmad Radzi said the case has been referred to the ministry's branch in Pasir Mas for further investigation under Section 16 of the Control of Supplies Act 1961.
"Initial investigations revealed that the suspect had been operating the business for nearly 13 years since 2013, supplying LPG cylinders to customers in the surrounding village area," he said.
He added that the woman admitted obtaining her supply from a local distributor and selling the cylinders at a profit without prior approval from the authorities.
"The GOF will continue to intensify monitoring and enforcement to curb the misuse and illegal storage of controlled goods in the area," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia to shift from Smart Cities to AI Cities, says Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is set to shift from Smart City development towards AI Cities as part of efforts to accelerate its ambition of becoming an artificial intelligence-driven nation, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
In a Facebook post, Anwar said he chaired a special meeting to review and coordinate the implementation of the AI City model for Malaysia, stressing that the country was now at a critical juncture in its digital transformation journey.
He said the transition would go beyond the adoption of new technology, requiring a comprehensive overhaul in how cities were planned, governed and managed to ensure they were more efficient, resilient and data-driven.
"This effort demands a strong ecosystem, including secure AI and data governance, high-capacity digital infrastructure, local talent development, as well as policy and legislative frameworks that support responsible innovation," he said.
Anwar added that the meeting agreed for the Digital Ministry to act as the main coordinating agency, working closely with other ministries and relevant agencies to ensure smooth implementation.
He said the government would continue to strengthen inter-agency collaboration to ensure high-impact delivery for the people, while reinforcing Malaysia's position as a competitive AI nation on the global stage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Batang Kali chicken farm ordered to close over fly infestation complaints
Article:
HULU SELANGOR: A chicken farm in Batang Kali has been ordered to close for 14 days after complaints of fly infestation affecting nearby residents.
The Hulu Selangor Municipal Council (MPHS) said the action was taken by the Environmental Control Unit, Solid Waste Management and Public Cleansing Corporation under By-Law 43 of the Trade, Business and Industries By-Laws.
The closure, from April 8 to 21, followed complaints believed to be linked to improper chicken waste management at the farm.
"The company is not allowed to bring in new chickens during the closure period until the site condition is deemed satisfactory.
"It is also required to submit a fly control plan and chicken waste management plan for evaluation," it said in a statement.
The council said monitoring would be carried out during the closure to ensure compliance and safeguard environmental cleanliness and public well-being.
As a long-term measure, the company has also been advised to consider relocating its poultry operations.
"MPHS remains committed to ensuring all business activities are conducted responsibly, in compliance with the law, and do not affect residents," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Detainee escapes during transfer at Sungai Buloh Prison; manhunt on
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Pakistani detainee, earlier reported to have fled custody, escaped during a transfer at Sungai Buloh Prison after attending court proceedings at the Shah Alam Court last night.
The Prisons Department said the incident occurred at 6pm on April 9 within the prison compound while the detainee was being transported following the conclusion of his trial.
"The Prisons Department confirms that the detainee managed to escape from custody after the completion of trial proceedings at the Shah Alam Court," it said in a statement today.
Authorities have since launched a manhunt, with the department working closely with police to track down and re-arrest the escapee.
"All security agencies have been alerted to assist in the effort," the statement added.
The department said it remained committed to ensuring the safety of detainees and the public, and would take firm action against any breach of procedures.
It was earlier
reported
that police had launched a manhunt for a remand detainee who escaped from Sungai Buloh Prison yesterday.
The Pakistani was being held for offences including robbery and the sexual assault of a minor.
Sungai Buloh district police chief Superintendent Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said the 24-year-old detainee was reported to have fled the facility yesterday.
"Efforts have been intensified in surrounding areas and through various other measures," he said in a statement.
The man, identified as Muhammad Hassan, is facing multiple charges, including under Section 377C and Section 392 of the Penal Code, Sections 14(a) and 14(d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, and Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63.
He was last seen wearing an orange T-shirt and black long trousers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] MACC widens probe into RM300mil share transaction scandal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has widened its probe into the RM300 million share transaction scandal, focusing on suspected overseas fund movements and questionable valuation practices linked to the deal.
The latest development comes a day after a former chief executive officer of a government-linked statutory body and a holding company chairman were remanded over their alleged roles in the transaction.
Sources said investigators from the MACC Special Operations Division conducted searches at two additional valuation firms today to verify the actual worth of the shares involved, amid suspicions of inflated pricing.
"Investigations indicate the valuation used in the transaction was unreasonable and disproportionate, which could have resulted in losses exceeding RM300 million in public funds.
"The probe has also uncovered elements suggesting the scheme was systematically planned, with suspected fund transfers involving overseas accounts," the source said.
So far, 10 individuals have had their statements recorded, with seven more expected to be called in soon.
MACC Special Operations Division senior director Datuk Mohamad Zamri Zainul Abidin confirmed that the investigation remains focused on offences under Section 16 of the MACC Act 2009, as well as criminal breach of trust.
He added that investigators are also examining other possible offences, particularly those related to money laundering activities.
The case is part of a broader probe into alleged
collusion
involving several board members and shareholders in the share sale transaction, which is believed to have caused significant losses to public funds.
It was reported that a former chief executive officer of a government-linked statutory body and the chairman of a holding company were remanded for four days over a share sale transaction that reportedly caused RM300 million in losses to public funds.
The remand, until April 12, was issued by magistrate Azeren Zakariah following an application by the MACC at the Putrajaya magistrate's court on April 9.
According to sources, the two men, in their 40s and 50s, were arrested at 6pm yesterday when they arrived to give statements at the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya.
Preliminary investigations found that both suspects are believed to have conspired with several board members and shareholders of a company in a share sale transaction involving the statutory body.
Initial findings also indicate that the transaction involved an unreasonable valuation, resulting in losses to public funds estimated to exceed RM300 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Farmer pleads guilty to slamming infant daughter, threatening to kill father
Article:
BATU PAHAT: "All this was caused by 'mushroom' (a vape liquid laced with synthetic drugs)," said a farmer after pleading guilty at the Sessions Court and magistrate's court here today to charges of injuring his infant daughter and threatening to kill his father.
The accused, Muhammad Shaffik Amat Zairudin, 29, who has one child, entered guilty pleas before judge Osman Affendi Mohd Shalleh and magistrate Arun Noval Dass.
At the magistrate's court, he pleaded guilty to two counts of criminal intimidation under Section 506 of the Penal Code for threatening to kill his 62-year-old father.
The incidents occurred at a house in Parit Raja at 10.30am and 11pm on April 7.
Based on the facts of the case, the accused became enraged after his father reprimanded him for allegedly taking prohibited substances. He then pushed his 13-month-old daughter's head before slamming her onto the floor.
He later threatened his father, saying: "Do you want to be killed?" and "Do not interfere with me, or I will kill everyone in this house."
In a fit of rage, he also brandished a knife at his father, leading to a struggle.
Police arrested the accused at 3am the following day at his home to assist in investigations.
The magistrate's court sentenced him to three and four months' imprisonment for the two charges, to run concurrently from April 8, the date of arrest.
Meanwhile, at the Sessions Court, he pleaded guilty to causing injury to his daughter under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001.
The offence carries a maximum penalty of a RM50,000 fine, up to 20 years' imprisonment, or both, upon conviction.
The court fixed May 22 for the submission of the medical report, facts of the case and sentencing. No bail was offered.
The prosecution was conducted by deputy public prosecutors Sharifah Natasha Syed Ahmad and Firdaus Ruslan, while the accused was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation counsel Tan Song Yan.
The accused previously served a three-month jail sentence in 2020 after pleading guilty to sending offensive messages insulting Islam.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 99pct backing for Penang Mutiara LRT signals strong public confidence – Loke
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Nearly 99 per cent of respondents have expressed support for the Penang Mutiara Line Light Rail Transit (LRT) project, reflecting strong public backing for the state's flagship transport initiative.
The encouraging response follows public inspection sessions for the proposed alignment linking Komtar and Penang Sentral, which have drawn more than 16,000 feedback submissions to date.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook said the strong show of support demonstrates clear public confidence in the project and its long-term benefits.
"This is not just a statistic, but a clear signal that the people of Penang recognise the importance of the Mutiara Line LRT project in addressing traffic congestion and improving daily mobility.
"The level of support also strengthens the resolve of both the federal and state governments to ensure the project is implemented efficiently and according to schedule," he said.
He spoke at the handover of the state's iconic Pesta site to Malaysia Rapid Transit Corporation Sdn Bhd.
Present were Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow and MRT Corporation chief executive officer Datuk Mohd Zarif Hashim.
Elaborating, Loke said public confidence would continue to grow as visible progress on the ground accelerates, particularly with the commencement of major construction works such as viaduct piers and elevated structures.
"As we have seen in other major infrastructure projects, once physical structures begin to take shape, it reinforces public trust that this is a project being delivered — not just planned.
"This momentum is important not only for public assurance, but also in boosting investor confidence and supporting economic activity along the corridor," he said.
Loke said the expanded alignment linking Komtar to Penang Sentral in Butterworth was a strategic decision to enhance connectivity between the island and the mainland, while supporting more balanced development across Penang.
He reiterated that the project, targeted for completion by 2031, would position Penang as the first city outside the Klang Valley to have a comprehensive LRT system, marking a significant milestone in the nation's public transport development.
Originally planned as an island-only service terminating at Komtar, the Mutiara Line was later expanded after the federal government took over the project.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim subsequently announced the extension of the alignment to Penang Sentral in Butterworth to strengthen interconnectivity and support balanced development across both sides of the state.
As part of the expansion, Sungai Nibong has been identified as the most suitable location for an additional depot following detailed evaluations.
The site, located near the midpoint of the alignment, will function as a train stabling yard and support overall system operations alongside the main depot at the Penang South Reclamation (PSR) area.
To preserve the site's heritage value — long associated with the Penang Pesta since 1966 — the depot design has been revised to reduce land use to about 11.5 acres.
The remaining areas will be retained for community use, with plans for upgrades and transit-oriented development (TOD).
Loke said the approach mirrors integrated developments in global cities such as Hong Kong, where rail depots are combined with residential and commercial projects.
Meanwhile, Loke said land acquisition for the project is progressing in stages.
For the first segment from Pematang Damar Laut to Macallum, 61 private land lots have undergone hearings under the Land Acquisition Act 1960, with compensation largely settled.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Malaysian vessel clears Strait of Hormuz, oil supply remains stable, says PM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Malaysian-owned vessel has successfully cleared the Strait of Hormuz and is now on its way home, with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim offering cautious reassurance that the country's oil supply for April and May remains secure.
"As for the other six, they are on their way, and with this, we hope, Insya-Allah, that oil supply for April and into May remains very much under control," he told reporters after Friday prayers with the public at Masjid Nurussaadah, Kampung Cheras Baru, Pandan, today.
Anwar said he was briefed on the latest developments by Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) president and group chief executive officer Tan Sri Tengku Muhammad Taufik Tengku Aziz earlier in the day.
On Tuesday, Iran's Ambassador to Malaysia Valiollah Mohammadi Nasrabadi said the vessels would be allowed to transit the waterway in stages.
This followed confirmation from the Foreign Ministry that
one of seven Malaysian-owned ships
previously stranded in the strait due to regional tensions had been granted passage and was continuing its journey to its destination.
Anwar expressed appreciation to Iran for facilitating the
transit of Malaysian oil tankers
through the strategic waterway, describing the move as important for safeguarding the country's energy security.
Vessel movements
in the Strait of Hormuz resumed on Wednesday, according to Xinhua, after Tehran agreed to temporarily reopen the passage as part of a two-week truce.
The development came after United States President Donald Trump announced a
ceasefire on Thursday,
saying Iran had submitted a "workable" 10-point proposal, with negotiations expected to determine the prospects of a longer-term agreement.
The situation unfolds amid heightened tensions in the Middle East following large-scale attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran since Feb 28.
In retaliation, Iran has targeted US military bases across the region, launched waves of missiles and drones at Israel, and disrupted nearly 20 per cent of global oil and liquefied natural gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz.
Editor's Note:
An earlier version of this report has been amended to reflect an update from the authorities that only one Malaysian vessel has cleared the Strait of Hormuz, while six remain in the strait.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Ministries study ways to ease impact of diesel price hike
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Several ministries are studying ways to ease the burden on sectors affected by rising diesel prices due to the ongoing Middle East crisis, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Anwar said assistance had already been provided to farmers, smallholders and fishermen, while efforts were being made to support other affected sectors.
"Where it concerns farmers, smallholders and fishermen, assistance has been provided — but for other sectors, several ministries are currently studying ways to ease the pressure on them," he said.
He added that the diesel subsidy would be maintained.
"There are those who feel burdened by the diesel price increase — the subsidy will be maintained so that people are not put under strain," he said.
Speaking to reporters after performing Friday prayers with the public at Masjid Nurussaadah, Kampung Cheras Baru in Pandan, here today, Anwar said the Cabinet was monitoring the energy situation daily through the Crisis Management Task Force (PPPK) to ensure it remained under control.
On RON95 fuel, the prime minister said the government remained committed to maintaining the price despite the financial pressure on public finances.
"For now, we are making every effort to maintain the RON95 price, even though it places some financial pressure on the government," he said.
He added that the public must also play its part by avoiding wasteful consumption.
"If there is room to save, it does not mean we must use it. Everyone has a role to play in safeguarding the nation's economy and its people," he said.
On Wednesday, the Finance Ministry said
diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia
had risen further under the latest fuel price revision, even as the government maintains targeted fuel subsidies to shield the public and key sectors from global oil market volatility.
The ministry said global crude prices had surged over recent weeks amid the Middle East crisis, pushing petroleum supply costs higher.
Vessel movements
resumed on Wednesday
in the Strait of Hormuz, according to Xinhua, after Tehran agreed to temporarily reopen the strait as part of a two-week truce.
This followed an announcement by United States President Donald Trump on Thursday, saying
Iran had presented a "workable" 10-point proposal
, with negotiations expected to determine whether a longer-term agreement can be reached.
The developments come amid escalating tensions in the Middle East following large-scale attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran since Feb 28.
In retaliation, Iran has targeted US military bases across the region, launched waves of missiles and drones at Israel, and disrupted the passage of about 20 per cent of global oil and liquefied natural gas through the Strait of Hormuz.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Residents near Sungai Buloh Prison on edge as manhunt continues for escaped detainee
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Residents near Sungai Buloh Prison are on edge following the escape of a remand prisoner, with many tightening security, keeping a close watch on their surroundings and staying alert for suspicious activity.
Police are intensifying efforts to track down the 24-year-old Pakistani, who fled the facility on April 9 and is wanted over a string of serious offences, including robbery and sexual crimes involving a minor.
For many, the escape has shattered a long-held sense of safety, replacing it with unease.
Defani Savaer, 39, who has lived in Bandar Seri Coalfields for nine years, said the incident has altered her sense of security.
"I didn't have concerns when I first moved here as I trusted the security measures in place," she said.
"Now it's worrying, especially given the seriousness of the offence and the fact that we have young children. We are staying vigilant and taking precautions."
She learnt about the escape earlier today through online news and neighbourhood group chats. Concern quickly spread among residents.
"We are worried as we are not sure of his whereabouts. Hopefully they can find him soon," she added.
Another resident, Anita, 41, who has lived in the area for 12 years, described the situation as "terrifying".
"I never thought much about living near a prison as escapes are rare in Malaysia," she said.
"I only found out this morning after my husband forwarded a news article to me."
Anita said her immediate concern was her child's safety, prompting her to alert school authorities.
"I informed my daughter's school right away. It is frightening to think he could be hiding somewhere near our homes," she said.
Police have urged the public to remain alert but calm as
operations to locate and re-arrest the detainee continue
.
Authorities said the escapee, identified as Muhammad Hassan, was last seen wearing an orange T-shirt and black long trousers.
Members of the public with information are urged to contact investigating officers or the nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Singapore's stance on Strait of Hormuz is its prerogative
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Singapore's decision not to negotiate with Iran over safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz is its own prerogative, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said the republic was entitled to its position, while stressing that Malaysia had adopted a clear and firm stance on the matter.
"That is Singapore's view — that is their affair. Our affair is to maintain good relations, and we have taken a fairly firm stance," he told reporters after performing Friday prayers with the public at Masjid Nurussaadah, Kampung Cheras Baru in Pandan today.
He said Malaysia's position was grounded in its broader assessment of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.
"We cannot discuss Iran's response (on closing the strait) without also addressing the injustice — the barbaric Israeli attacks on Iran, backed by the United States. Our foreign policy position on this is clear," he said.
However, Anwar drew a distinction between Malaysia's stance on the issue and its diplomatic relations with neighbouring countries.
"But our friendship with neighbouring countries must also be maintained," he added.
On Tuesday, Singapore Foreign Minister Dr Vivian Balakrishnan said the city-state would
not engage in negotiations with Iran
over safe passage for ships or toll arrangements in the Strait of Hormuz, in line with its principle of not taking sides.
He said Singapore regarded passage through the strait as a right of all nations rather than a privilege granted by bordering states, citing Article 44 of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS).
Vessel movements through the waterway resumed on Wednesday, according to Xinhua, after Tehran agreed to a
temporary reopening
as part of a two-week truce.
While Iran has
agreed to reopen
the strategic route during the truce, it has also mooted a toll system to fund reconstruction following US-Israeli strikes.
The developments come amid escalating tensions in the Middle East following large-scale attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran since Feb 28.
In retaliation, Iran has targeted US military bases across the region, launched waves of missiles and drones at Israel, and disrupted nearly 20 per cent of global oil and liquefied natural gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Axiata unit secures US$50mil from Maybank subsidiary for Cambodian digital push
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Maybank Cambodia has extended a US$50 million financing to Axiata Group Bhd's unit Smart Axiata to support the expansion of Cambodia's digital and telecommunications infrastructure including 5G connectivity.
The financing will help strengthen Cambodia's digital backbone, improving access to digital education, remote learning and financial services, while enabling small and medium enterprises (SMEs) to adopt technology that can enhance productivity and expand market reach.
Maybank Cambodia and Smart Axiata said they will continue to deepen their collaboration to advance Cambodia's connectivity-driven development and support broader Asean regional progress.
Maybank Cambodia chief executive officer and country head Rath Sophoan said the financing facilitates community development and the digital economy in line with Cambodia's Digital Economy and Society Policy Framework 2021 to 2035 and Maybank's ROAR30 sustainability and new economy priorities.
"We view the new economy as a core pillar of Cambodia's national agenda, in addition to being a key driver of Asean's growth to accelerate the shift toward high-impact, high-value sectors.
"This collaboration with Smart Axiata represents our first new economy-aligned financing in Cambodia, underscoring Maybank group's ROAR30 strategic plan to mobilise RM100 billion (US$25 billion) in new economy initiatives and RM300 billion (US$75 billion) in sustainable finance by 2030," he said in a statement.
Smart Axiata chief executive officer Ritesh Kumar Singh said connectivity remains central to the company's mission of empowering people and supporting Cambodia's long-term growth.
He added that the financing from Maybank Cambodia allows the company to accelerate investments in 5G and digital infrastructure, expanding access so more Cambodians can learn, work and grow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] 34 men fined for rioting at Cheras entertainment outlet
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Thirty-four men involved in a violent attack at an entertainment outlet in Cheras were fined between RM2,000 and RM3,000 by the magistrate's court today.
Magistrate Faezahnoor Hassan handed down the sentence after all the accused, aged between 19 and 57, pleaded guilty to rioting while armed with deadly weapons last week.
Thirty-two of the accused were fined RM3,000 each, in default four months' imprisonment. They are Tan Chee Chin, G. Bathmanathan, M. Kogilan, Lim Puay Fei, Kevin Yong, Ong Zhi Jian, Looi Khai Xuan, Voison Chor, Tan Jun Ning, Hee Tuck Choy, Ching Thong Hai, Kee Jin Xuan, Lim Teong Yow, Chan Kuang Man and Ho Chee Seong.
Also in the group were Wong Chee Siong, Liang Chung Weng, G. Srikanth, A. Ganesan, Lee Ming Choong, Wong Wai Khuen, Khor Ka Lok, Ong Zheng Yuan, Yam Hao Jie, Lim Jia Cheng, Chai Chun You, Kher Kuan Heng, Ong Aik Chim, Chong Khai Jun, Hadzrin Julasrin, Lee Tuck Wah and Chin Chin Chung.
Meanwhile, two young offenders, Alvin Yong and Chong Yu Heng, both aged 19, were fined RM2,000 each, in default three months' imprisonment.
According to the charge sheet, the accused had taken part in a riot and were armed with weapons, ammunition, explosives, sticks and stones that could be used in an attack.
The incident occurred at an entertainment outlet in Cheras at 7.30pm on April 5.
The offence falls under Section 148 of the Penal Code, which provides for a maximum imprisonment of five years, or a fine, or both, upon conviction.
Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Nur Farhana Mohamad Poad urged the court to impose a deterrent sentence.
Lawyer Mohammad Arifin Abdul Wahab, who represented all the accused, pleaded for a lenient sentence, saying they were first-time offenders.
"The men are seeking a second chance. This is their first offence and they apologise for their actions," he said.
Based on the facts of the case, police received a report of a fight involving the use of a lorry and the ramming of a gate at 7.30pm on April 5.
A group of men caused damage to property in the area, with investigations revealing that some suspects used weapons during the attack.
A 60-second video clip circulating online showed a lorry reversing and crashing into the gate of the premises, before a group of men, their faces covered with skull caps and armed with unidentified objects, broke into the premises.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Focus on economy, not Rafizi row
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged calm over growing calls within PKR for action to be taken against Pandan member of parliament Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli.
Anwar, who is also party president, dismissed the internal party row as a personal matter unworthy of attention amid more pressing concerns.
"Let us remain calm and focus on the country's economic problems, which are far more serious than personal matters," he said.
He was speaking to reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid Nurussaadah, Kampung Cheras Baru, Pandan.
Earlier, Rafizi said he had submitted a
formal reply
to a second show-cause letter from the PKR disciplinary board dated April 9, defending his remarks and denying the allegations.
Rafizi, who is also the Member of Parliament for Pandan, said his criticism of what he described as a "culture of sycophancy" in the party was not new and had been raised with party leadership at previous congresses.
On Tuesday, PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said
calls for action
against Rafizi would be discussed by the disciplinary board.
This followed warnings from state party leaders that his remarks could spark controversy and spread misleading narratives, potentially undermining government effectiveness and party stability.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azalina: Second meeting on separating A-G, prosecutor roles examined various views
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 (Bernama) – Efforts to separate the roles of Attorney General (AG) and Public Prosecutor are being strengthened through engagement sessions with multiple parties, including non-governmental organisations and civil society.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform), Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, said at its second meeting yesterday, Parliament's Special Select Committee examined various views, particularly on the appointment, termination and accountability of the Public Prosecutor.
"Yesterday I chaired the second meeting of the Special Select Committee on the separation of the A-G and Public Prosecutor's roles to advance institutional reform towards greater transparency and integrity," she said in a Facebook post.
She said engagement sessions will continue to ensure all views are considered in efforts to trengthen the nation's justice system.
"The Madani government is committed to realising this agenda because it is crucial for strengthening public confidence," she said. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's March palm oil stocks hit seven-month low as exports surge
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's palm oil inventories slid in March, down for a third straight month and hitting a seven-month low on a surge in exports that more than offset a modest increase in output.
Falling inventories in the world's second-largest producer of the tropical oil could support benchmark Malaysian futures , which have climbed in recent weeks after the Iran conflict drove energy prices higher.
Malaysia's palm oil stocks tumbled 16.1 per cent to 2.27 million metric tons in March from February, marking their lowest level since August, data from the Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) showed on Friday.
Inventories were slightly higher than expectations. Crude palm oil production increased 7.2 per cent to 1.38 million tons, snapping a four-month run of declines. Even so, that was the lowest level of output for the month of March in three years.
Palm oil exports rocketed 40.7 per cent higher to 1.55 million tons, the most in five months. A Reuters survey had forecast inventories at 2.18 million tons, with output seen at 1.34 million tons and exports at 1.56 million tons.
"Despite the hefty reduction in March, Malaysian palm oil inventories remain well above typical levels for this time of year," said Anilkumar Bagani, research head at vegetable oil broker Sunvin Group.
"Export performance will need to be repeated in April to support the market, as production so far this month is still seen as elevated, while exports are sharply down in the first 10 days."
A recent rally in soyoil prices has widened palm oil's discount to its rival, potentially drawing Indian buyers back into the market after they stayed on the sidelines last month, said a New Delhi-based dealer with a global trade house.
India's palm oil imports fell nearly 19 per cent in March to a three-month low, dealers said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM Anwar: Malaysia at turning point to shift from smart cities to AI cities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Malaysia is now at a critical juncture to transition from Smart City development to Artificial Intelligence (AI) City development as the foundation for its aspiration to become an AI Nation, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
The Prime Minister said this was emphasised during a special meeting he chaired this morning at the Prime Minister’s Office to review and coordinate the implementation of an AI City model for Malaysia.
He said the shift is not merely about adopting new technologies but requires a comprehensive transformation in how cities are planned, governed and managed to be more efficient, resilient and data-driven.
“This effort requires a strong ecosystem, encompassing secure AI and data governance, high-capacity digital infrastructure, local talent development, as well as policy and legal frameworks that support responsible innovation.
“The meeting also agreed that the Digital Ministry will act as the main coordinator, working closely with relevant ministries and agencies,” he said in a Facebook post.
Anwar said the government will continue to strengthen this synergy to ensure effective and high-impact implementation for the overall benefit of the people, while reinforcing Malaysia’s position as a globally competitive AI Nation.
Previously, Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said Malaysia is actively implementing the National AI Action Plan to achieve its aspiration of becoming an AI Nation by 2030.
He said 2026 will be a key year in advancing this goal, with a focus on developing technology, talent, governance, infrastructure and sustainable investment. — Bernama

Title: Langkawi ferry operator raises fares for non-MyKad holders by RM10 amid rising costs
Article:
ALOR SETAR, April 10 — Langkawi Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd has announced a RM10 fare increase for non-MyKad passengers, effective April 15, 2026.
Its general manager, Dr Baharin Baharom, said the fare adjustment is necessary due to ongoing and significant industry cost increases which the company can no longer absorb.
"Over the years, the ferry industry has experienced substantial increases in industrial fuel prices, maintenance, manpower, and regulatory compliance costs, while Langkawi ferry fares have not reflected these rising operational expenses.
"Continuing operations under these conditions is no longer viable. Without this adjustment, the industry risks service reductions, operational disruptions, and long-term sustainability challenges that would ultimately affect users,” he said in a statement today.
After the adjustment, non-MyKad passenger fares from Kuala Perlis to Langkawi will be RM45 one way for adults and RM35 for children. From Kuala Kedah, fares will be RM55 for adults and RM42 for children.
Baharin said the adjustment does not affect Malaysians with a MyKad, as maintaining affordability and accessibility for locals remains a top priority.
Fares for Malaysian citizens remain unchanged at RM21 (adult) and RM16 (child) from Kuala Perlis, and RM26.50 (adult) and RM20.50 (child) from Kuala Kedah.
Senior citizens are charged RM13 from Kuala Perlis and RM17 from Kuala Kedah, while persons with disabilities (PwD) pay RM10 from Kuala Perlis and RM14 from Kuala Kedah.
He said the adjustment is reasonable for non-MyKad passengers, as Langkawi ferry fares remain significantly lower than those on comparable island routes in Malaysia.
"For comparison, ferry fares to Tioman is RM85 despite covering a similar nautical distance. This demonstrates that even after the adjustment, Langkawi ferry fares remain well below prevailing market levels," he said.
He added that the operational continuity and safety standards will not be compromised, and that this measure is essential to ensure ferry services continue to operate safely, reliably, and sustainably. — Bernama

Title: Paddy farmers seek stronger government support as costs rise, food security at risk
Article:
ALOR SETAR, April 10 — Pertubuhan Persaudaraan Pesawah Malaysia (PeSAWAH), an organisation that represents and advocates paddy farmers, has called for stronger and sustained government support to help the group cope with rising production costs and ensure the sustainability of the country’s rice industry.
Its chairman, Abdul Rashid Yob, said the increase in fuel prices has significantly impacted farm operating costs, necessitating a comprehensive approach to ensure the sector remains sustainable.
He said the cost of paddy cultivation has risen sharply compared with three decades ago, involving increases in various aspects, including ploughing and harvesting services as well as agricultural inputs such as fertilisers and pesticides.
“We understand that global factors such as conflicts and crises are contributing to rising costs, but immediate solutions and prompt action from the government are crucial as paddy cultivation must continue consistently.
“If local paddy production is disrupted or farmers decide to stop cultivating their fields, it will worsen the food security situation. Therefore, urgent government intervention is vital to support farmers and ensure the sustainability of the paddy industry,” he told reporters after a meeting session with farmers from Perlis, Penang and Perak here today.
Abdul Rashid said that farmers are placing high hopes on the government to introduce effective mechanisms to ease the burden of rising operational costs, as the sector not only serves as their primary source of income but also underpins national food security.
Meanwhile, PeSAWAH deputy chairman Azahar Hashim said higher fuel prices have also affected machinery operators, including those handling tractors and harvesting machines, which are critical components in the paddy production chain.
He said adjustments to service charges have had to be made from time to time to ensure operations can continue in line with current costs.
In a related development, tractor owner Mohd Amirul Idris said he is now more cautious in setting service rates following fluctuations in fuel prices.
“Despite the challenges, we remain committed to not burden farmers and to ensure that field operations continue smoothly,” he added. — Bernama

Title: Federal Court to hear PKR appeal over Zuraida’s RM100,000 bond ruling on July 16
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 10 — The Federal Court has fixed July 16 to hear Parti Keadilan Rakyat’s (PKR) appeal against a Court of Appeal decision that reduced former Ampang MP Datuk Zuraida Kamaruddin's bond payment from RM10 million to RM100,000 in a party bond breach case.
The hearing date was set during case management before Federal Court senior assistant registrar Syahrul Md Sain on Wednesday. PKR's lawyer, Navpreet Singh, confirmed the date when contacted by Bernama.
He added that all parties have been directed to file their written submissions by July 1. A final case management has also been scheduled for July 2 to update on pre-hearing compliance.
On Tuesday, the Federal Court dismissed Zuraida's application to review its earlier decision granting PKR leave to appeal the appellate court's ruling.
PKR obtained leave to proceed with the appeal on June 24, 2024.
On December 11, 2024, a three-judge panel of the Court of Appeal upheld the High Court's finding that Zuraida had breached the bond but reduced the quantum payable from RM10 million to RM100,000.
Earlier, on June 23, 2023, the Kuala Lumpur High Court ruled in favour of PKR and ordered Zuraida to pay RM10 million in accordance with the bond's terms. The presiding judge, Datuk Akhtar Tahir, held that the bond was valid and binding.
The lawsuit was initiated in 2020 by PKR's secretary-general, Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, on behalf of the party.
It claimed that Zuraida had violated the terms of a bond she signed before contesting in the 14th General Election in 2018.
Under the bond, Zuraida was required to pay PKR RM10 million within seven days if she resigned from the party, joined another political party, or became an independent representative after being elected on the party's ticket.
In her defence, Zuraida contended that she was forced to sign the bond to be eligible to stand as a PKR candidate in the general election. — Bernama

Title: Sack Rafizi? Anwar says focus on economy problem more important now
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today brushed aside questions over Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli’s position in the party, saying attention should instead be focused on managing the economy.
Anwar, who is also prime minister and finance minister, was asked to respond to Rafizi’s challenge for PKR to sack him following a second show-cause letter over alleged breaches of party disciplinary and ethical rules.
“Nevermind, I just think everyone should calm down as focusing on the economic problem is more important than on a personal problem,” he replied to reporters after afternoon Friday prayers at a mosque in Cheras.
This morning Rafizi said he had received a second show-cause letter from disciplinary board chairman Datuk Zainol Samah, who alleged the Pandan MP had repeatedly made negative statements against the party and Anwar.
Among the claims cited was Rafizi’s alleged criticism of Anwar, including remarks that the prime minister expects subordinates to constantly praise him and follow his instructions.
The board also accused Rafizi of undermining the party’s image by alleging Anwar was defending Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief Tan Sri Azam Baki.
Rafizi has maintained his challenge for the party to sack him if it believes he has violated party rules.

Title: EPF: Akaun Fleksibel withdrawals largely for short-term survival, but gap seen across income groups
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Malaysians tapping into their Employees Provident Fund (EPF) savings under the Akaun Fleksibel facility are largely using the money to cover daily necessities, healthcare and debt repayments rather than discretionary spending, according to the pension fund’s latest report.
A recent EPF report titled "Enduring Today, Shaping Tomorrow: Akaun Fleksibel as a Financial Lifeline for Wellbeing" said the withdrawal patterns point to growing financial pressures, with members relying on their retirement savings to manage recurring expenses and short-term cash flow needs.
A survey of 14,204 members who withdrew from Akaun Fleksibel between May 2024 and June 2025 found that most withdrawals were for essential and emergency needs. About 93 per cent used funds for daily necessities, 81.7 per cent for emergency-related expenses, 74.4 per cent for debt and investment obligations, and 73.3 per cent for seasonal or lifestyle costs.
The most common expenditure was on food and daily necessities like groceries and infant formula and diapers, cited by 70.3 per cent of members. Health treatments accounted for 58.7 per cent, while debt payments represented 56.6 per cent. Only 18.3 per cent of members reported using funds for lifestyle purposes, indicating discretionary spending was not a primary driver.
“This relatively low share suggests that discretionary spending was not a primary motivation for withdrawals, with usage largely focused on essential and risk-mitigating expenditures rather than non-essential consumption,” said the report.
The report found that most withdrawals are relatively small and regular, indicating that members are using the facility as a supplementary income stream rather than for one-off large expenditures.
Usage patterns also varied by withdrawal frequency. High-frequency withdrawers, active in nine or more months during the period, tended to use Akaun Fleksibel for routine and seasonal needs, such as food and commuting costs.
Low-frequency withdrawers, active in one or two months, typically reserved the facility for episodic or emergency expenses, including job loss, business setbacks, entrepreneurial activities, or major home maintenance.
Demographics of withdrawal spending
